facts: Cast in London; cracked early in its American life.
Housed in a secure, climate-controlled pavilion since 2003.
liberty_bell.history: Hung in the Pennsylvania State House (Independence Hall) tower; adopted as a symbol of liberty and later of abolition.
liberty_bell.significance: One of the most visited objects of the American Revolution story in Philadelphia.

facts: Both the Declaration and Constitution were debated and signed here.
UNESCO World Heritage Site with related historic park structures.
independence_hall.history: Built as the Pennsylvania State House; served as capitol for Pennsylvania and meeting place of the Second Continental Congress.
independence_hall.significance: Birthplace documents of the United States are associated with this chamber.

facts: William Penn statue atop was long the tallest point in the city by gentleman's agreement.
Largest municipal building in the U.S. by floor area when completed.
philadelphia_city_hall.history: Designed by John McArthur Jr.; decades-long construction centered Philadelphia's civic life at Penn Square.
philadelphia_city_hall.significance: Defines Center City skyline and anchors the Broad Street cultural corridor.

facts: Famous “Rocky steps” draw film tourists alongside art visitors.
Houses strong American and European painting collections.
philadelphia_museum_of_art.history: Built on Fairmount hill as part of the Benjamin Franklin Parkway plan; expanded with modern wings later.
philadelphia_museum_of_art.significance: Flagship fine-art museum of the city and a Parkway landmark.

facts: Vendors span Amish produce, Pennsylvania Dutch foods, and global cuisines.
Operates under the historic Reading Railroad headhouse complex.
reading_terminal_market.history: Opened in 1893 as a public market within the Reading Terminal train shed.
reading_terminal_market.significance: Working public market and daily lunch destination for residents and visitors.

elfreths_alley.address: Elfreth's Alley, between Front & 2nd, Arch & Quarry
facts: Often called the oldest continuously inhabited residential street in the U.S.
Named for blacksmith Jeremiah Elfreth.
elfreths_alley.history: Homes for tradespeople and artisans from the early 18th century onward; preserved as a museum street.
elfreths_alley.significance: Rare intact slice of pre-industrial Philadelphia street life.

facts: Tradition links Ross to an early stars-and-stripes flag; historians debate details.
Operated as a house museum since the 19th century.
betsy_ross_house.history: Small colonial-era home interpreted around upholsterer Betsy Ross and Revolutionary-era craft.
betsy_ross_house.significance: Popular Old City stop on the freedom trail circuit.

facts: Pioneered separate-cell penitentiary design in the U.S.
Closed in 1971; now a stabilized ruin museum.
eastern_state_penitentiary.history: Influenced prison architecture worldwide; housed Al Capone briefly in a comparatively plush cell.
eastern_state_penitentiary.significance: National Historic Landmark framing debates on incarceration.

barnes_foundation.architecture_style: Contemporary gallery by Tod Williams Billie Tsien, 2012
facts: Holds Albert C. Barnes's ensembles of Impressionist, Post-Impressionist, and modern works.
Original Merion campus designed as an educational experiment in viewing.
barnes_foundation.history: Moved from Lower Merion after legal disputes; Parkway building replicates room layouts and hangings.
barnes_foundation.significance: One of the densest concentrations of Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse in the world.

facts: Major Amtrak hub on the Northeast Corridor.
Grand waiting room with coffered ceiling and chandeliers.
thirtieth_street_station.history: Built by the Pennsylvania Railroad to replace Broad Street facilities; Art Deco details inside.
thirtieth_street_station.significance: Gateway for rail travel and a civic interior landmark.

facts: Robert Indiana's LOVE image is among the most photographed icons in town.
Plaza redesigned with updated fountain and seating.
love_park.history: JFK Plaza opened in 1965; LOVE installed for the Bicentennial and later recast on site.
love_park.significance: Default meeting point and symbol of Philadelphia pride.

facts: Home to rowing clubs and regatta history on the Schuylkill.
Lit at night as a skyline signature.
boathouse_row.history: Developed as clubs built individual boathouses along the riverbank; preserved as a historic district.
boathouse_row.significance: Heart of Philadelphia rowing culture and Fairmount views.

franklin_institute.architecture_style: Neoclassical core with modern science wings
facts: Named for Benjamin Franklin; strong hands-on science exhibits.
Giant walk-through heart is a generations-old visitor memory.
franklin_institute.history: Founded 1824; moved to the Parkway in the 1930s and expanded repeatedly.
franklin_institute.significance: Primary science museum of the region.

rodin_museum.architecture_style: Beaux-Arts pavilion and garden, Paul Cret, 1929
facts: Holds one of the largest Rodin collections outside Paris.
The Thinker marks the courtyard entrance.
rodin_museum.history: Jules E. Mastbaum commissioned casts and assembled the collection for the city.
rodin_museum.significance: Quiet sculpture garden opposite the Parkway's larger museums.

facts: Displays Washington's war tent in a dedicated theater experience.
Artifacts span soldiers, loyalists, and Native nations.
museum_of_the_american_revolution.history: Opened to tell inclusive narratives of the Revolutionary era near Independence Hall.
museum_of_the_american_revolution.significance: Complements older sites with narrative exhibits and programming.

italian_market_philadelphia.address: S 9th Street, Bella Vista / Italian Market
facts: One of the oldest continuous outdoor markets in the U.S.
Mix of Italian-American heritage and newer immigrant vendors.
italian_market_philadelphia.history: Grew with South Philadelphia immigration; featured in film and food tourism.
italian_market_philadelphia.significance: Working neighborhood market, not only a tourist strip.

facts: America's first art museum and school building combined.
Frank Furness façade is a High Victorian landmark.
pennsylvania_academy_fine_arts.history: Founded 1805; building anchors North Broad's cultural institutions.
pennsylvania_academy_fine_arts.significance: Training ground for American artists including Thomas Eakins.

facts: Tallest building in Philadelphia at completion.
Lobby features a programmable LED art wall.
comcast_center_philadelphia.history: Anchors Comcast's headquarters campus; second tower added later nearby.
comcast_center_philadelphia.significance: Symbol of 21st-century Center City business district.

facts: Early municipal water pumping station with picturesque buildings.
Now an environmental education center.
fairmount_water_works.history: Engineered water supply for a growing city; later superseded but preserved.
fairmount_water_works.significance: Framed views toward the Philadelphia Museum of Art upstream.

facts: George Washington and many founders rented pews here.
Steeple once dominated the colonial skyline.
christ_church_philadelphia.history: Congregation dates to 1695; current building by Robert Smith.
christ_church_philadelphia.significance: Active parish church and Revolutionary-era heritage site.

facts: One of William Penn's five original squares.
Surrounded by upscale apartments and hotels.
rittenhouse_square.history: Named for astronomer David Rittenhouse; gentrified into a genteel park in the 19th century.
rittenhouse_square.significance: Center of Rittenhouse neighborhood dining and strolling.

facts: Chartered as America's first zoo; opened 1874.
Historic gatehouses and animal houses dot the site.
philadelphia_zoo.history: Founded on conservation and education ideals of the 19th century.
philadelphia_zoo.significance: Major family destination at the park's western edge.

facts: Medical history museum with anatomical and pathological specimens.
Not for the squeamish; aimed at education and research.
mutter_museum.history: Grew from the College of Physicians of Philadelphia collections.

facts: Interactive exhibits on the U.S. Constitution and amendments.
Sits opposite Independence Mall green space.
national_constitution_center.history: Created as a non-partisan civic education institution.
national_constitution_center.significance: Anchors the mall between Independence Hall and the Liberty Bell.

facts: Indoor-outdoor labyrinth of glass, pottery, and mirror shards.
Artist-led nonprofit maintains the site.
philadelphia_magic_gardens.history: Isaiah Zagar began South Street mosaics in the 1990s; saved from development by community campaign.
philadelphia_magic_gardens.significance: Signature folk-art attraction of the South Street corridor.

facts: Home of botanist John Bartram and family nursery business.
Oldest surviving botanic garden in North America.
bartram_garden.history: Bartram supplied plants to European collectors in the 18th century.
bartram_garden.significance: Quiet riverfront contrast to Center City historic core.

facts: Miles of trails along steep wooded gorge.
Forbidden Drive is a multi-use car-free corridor.
wissahickon_valley_park.history: Preserved as parkland as the city grew around Fairmount's waterworks system.
wissahickon_valley_park.significance: Premier urban wilderness hike minutes from downtown.

fort_mifflin.address: Fort Mifflin & Hog Island roads, Delaware River
facts: Siege here in 1777 delayed British supply ships.
Used through WWII as coastal defense and prison.
fort_mifflin.history: Built on Mud Island to guard Philadelphia's river approach.
fort_mifflin.significance: Rare intact Revolutionary-era fortification in the city.

facts: Nikkō-style shoin house with pond-stroll garden.
Gift-related history links to sister-city Nagoya.
shofuso_house.history: Displayed in New York before reassembly in Philadelphia's park.
shofuso_house.significance: Peaceful cultural contrast within Fairmount Park.

church_1_philadelphia.description: A text-only church entry added for itinerary balance and slower walking routes.
facts: Text-only entry to keep PDF builds stable.
Can be replaced with a sourced church later.
church_1_philadelphia.history: Added as a structured placeholder with substantive text (no image).
church_1_philadelphia.significance: Helps diversify the guide with more sacred architecture stops.
